title: madhuca erythrophylla
intro: Madhuca erythrophylla, also known as the forest butter tree, is a species of tree that is native to the tropical forests of the Western Ghats in India. The medicinal properties of Madhuca erythrophylla include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Madhuca erythrophylla can be made by boiling its leaves and bark in water or by extracting its oil, which can then be used to treat various health conditions.
